nodes.label: Unit tests for :class:`ServerMcpPool`.  Covers the non-trivial logic in the AP-s
HTTP MCPServerConfig with an optional tools allow-list.      ``MCPServerConfig``
AgentSpec with the given MCPServerConfigs and nothing else.      :param configs:
AgentSpec with no MCP servers.      :returns: A minimal :class:`AgentSpec` with
Minimal MCP tool definition.      :param name: Tool name, e.g. ``"search"``.
Stand-in for McpServerConnection; records connect / close / call_tool.      :par
Simulate connect; either raise or return tools.          :returns: The canned to
Return a scripted result for *name*, or a default stub.          :param name: To
Patch ``McpServerConnection`` in the mcp_pool module with _FakeConn stubs.
``list_tools`` must return an empty list when spec has no MCP servers.      Fail
``list_tools`` must return McpToolEntry objects for each tool on each server.
The ``tools`` allow-list on MCPServerConfig must filter returned tools.      Fai
Tools with invalid names (containing spaces) must be silently skipped.      Fail
A server that fails to connect must be skipped; healthy servers still surface to
``call_tool`` must route to the correct server and return its output.      Failu
``call_tool`` must raise RuntimeError when the spec has no MCP servers.      Fai
``call_tool`` must raise RuntimeError when the server name is not in the spec.
``call_tool`` must raise RuntimeError when the server failed to connect.      Fa
``shutdown_for`` must remove the agent's pool entry and close connections.
``shutdown_for`` must be a no-op when the agent has no pool entry.      Failure
``shutdown_all`` must close every live connection and empty the pool.      Failu
Changing the spec's MCP servers must evict the old pool entry and reconnect.
When the pool reaches capacity, the LRU agent's entry must be evicted.      Fail
